name: Scaloppine Al Lemon
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Veal scallops, cut into 3/8-inch thick slices
3/4 cup Beef stock, fresh or canned
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the veal scallops with pepper.
2. Dip them in flour and shake off excess.
3. In a heavy skillet, melt margarine with olive oil over moderate heat.
4. Add veal scallops four or five at a time and sauté until golden brown. Transfer the veal scallops to a plate. Pour off most of the fat, leaving a thin film on the bottom.
5. Add 3/4 cup beef stock and let boil briskly for a few minutes, stirring constantly. Scrape in any browned bits clinging to the bottom and sides of the pan.
6. Return the veal to the skillet and arrange lemon slices on top. Cover skillet and simmer over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes, or until veal is tender when pierced with a knife.
7. Transfer scallops to a heated platter and surround with lemon slices.
8. Add the remaining beef stock (1/4 cup) to the juices in the skillet and oil briskly until the stock turns to a syrupy glaze. Add lemon juice and cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Pour sauce over scallops.
Serves 6.
Note: Veal cubes (stewing meat) may be substituted. Pound as flat as possible. Follow directions above, but cooking time will be increased to 45 minutes to an hour over low heat or until tender.
